name: @radzor/kv-store
description: Key-value store with TTL support. Operates in-memory by default with optional Redis backend via raw RESP protocol. Supports expiration callbacks and namespace isolation.

inputs.name: redisUrl
inputs.description: Redis connection URL (required when mode is 'redis').

inputs.name: namespace
inputs.description: Key prefix for namespace isolation (default: none).

inputs.name: defaultTtl
inputs.description: Default TTL in milliseconds (0 = no expiry, default: 0).

outputs.name: storeStats
outputs.type: { size: number; mode: string; namespace: string }

fields.name: size
fields.description: Number of keys in the store.

events.name: onExpired
events.description: Fired when a key expires (in-memory mode only, checked on access or sweep).

actions.name: get
actions.description: Get a value by key. Returns null if not found or expired.

actions.name: set
actions.description: Set a key-value pair with optional TTL.

actions.name: delete
actions.description: Delete a key. Returns true if it existed.

actions.name: has
actions.description: Check if a key exists and is not expired.

actions.name: keys
actions.description: List all keys matching a pattern.

actions.name: clear
actions.description: Delete all keys in the current namespace.

llm.constraints: In-memory mode requires no dependencies and is lost on process restart. Redis mode uses raw RESP protocol over TCP (no npm packages). TTL is in milliseconds. Namespace prefixes are applied transparently — keys() returns unprefixed keys.
